data.description:
## Context (parking lot — undesigned; promote only if the serial treadmill is a MEASURED pain)

The auto-lander epic (f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af) Phase 1 is a SERIAL lander: ~1 change per CI-duration, so
under bursts of independent ready changes the wall-clock is N × CI-time (the FFO rebase-treadmill —
observed live: a docs change took 3 rebases to land). Phase-2 addresses throughput.

Cross-stack BATCHING (combine N changes → one CI run → land together) was evaluated by a design
spike and REJECTED on this infra: (1) the gerrit-to-platform bridge fires CI per-patchset with no
batch awareness, so assembling a batch = N runs on N partial trees, not one; (2) landing N off one
run requires the bot to direct-cast Verified on member patchsets from a combined-tree run — a
GerriScary / CVE-2025-1568-class posture regression; (3) it only wins in rare bursts of ≥4 and pays
a bisection tax + WIP-hook fragility.

The recommended throughput approach is BOUNDED SPECULATION: speculatively pre-run CI on the next few
queued changes (each rebased onto the projected tip) IN PARALLEL, up to the GHA concurrency cap (~3),
landing in order. Each candidate is a clean single-change-on-tip verified on its own tree, so NONE of
the batching gaps exist (no combined tree, no direct-cast, no g2p change). Wall-clock for 6 ≈ 28 min
(2 rounds) vs 84 serial vs batching's rare 14-min-but-fragile. Serial (Phase 1) is the fallback.

## Why an idea (not open)
Deferred until the serial treadmill is a MEASURED pain (validate against the Phase-1 queue-wait
metric). Promote + design then. Aligns with ADR-0040's "revisit a lightweight commit-queue bot if
the treadmill becomes real pain." Supersedes the retired batching stories S7/S8/S9 of epic f1fa.

## Rough acceptance (when promoted — to be designed)
- [ ] Speculate up to the concurrency cap on the next queued changes (each rebased onto the projected tip); land in order.
- [ ] Each candidate is an ordinary single change on tip, verified on its own tree (no combined tree, no bot-cast-Verified, no g2p change).
- [ ] Serial (Phase 1) is the fallback when speculation cannot apply.
- [ ] Driven by a real measured throughput pain (the Phase-1 wait metric), not speculatively.
data.title: Phase-2 throughput: bounded speculation (deferred; batching rejected by design spike)
